creator: draco@marino.st
text:
Okay, I re-ran this with -V3 argument.
It seems the reported error message is false.  Here's the bit that fails:

==============================
("/usr/bin/gcc",["-Wl,--hash-size=31","-Wl,--reduce-memory-overheads","/tmp/2066.c","-o","/tmp/2066","-ljavascriptcoregtk-1.0","-L/usr/local/lib","-pthread","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/gtk-0.12.4","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/pango-0.12.4","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/process-1.1.0.1","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/pretty-1.1.1.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/gio-0.12.4","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/glib-0.12.4","-L/usr/local/lib","- L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/directory-1.1.0.2","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/unix-2.5.1.1","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/old-time-1.1.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/old-locale-1.0.0.4","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/filepath-1.3.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/containers-0.4.2.1","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/deepseq-1.3.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/cairo-0.12.4","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/cabal/mtl-2.1.2","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/
 cabal/t
ransformers-0.3.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/bytestring-0.9.2.1","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/array-0.4.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/base-4.5.1.0","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/integer-gmp-0.4.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2/ghc-prim-0.2.0.0","-L/usr/local/lib/ghc-7.4.2"])
/usr/bin/gcc returned ExitFailure 1 with error message:
/usr/local/lib/libicui18n.so.50: undefined reference to `_Unwind_Resume'
==============================

The first thing to note is libicu18n.so.50 is built by gcc-4.4 and the 
/usr/bin/gcc here is gcc-4.7.  They both should be supporting 
dl-iterate-phdr for the unwind and they don't require libgcc_s for this. 
  Maybe ghc is overriding gcc specs to pull in libgcc_pic.a which has 
this _Unwind_Resume symbol?
